raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "Assembly Bill 705 codifies the use of high school transcript data as a student success indicator in California.",
    "Several states, including Connecticut and North Carolina, have implemented multiple measures programs with success.",
    "The sole use of placement tests has served as a barrier for students entering community college and receiving their diploma.",
    "Using multiple measures, including high school grades and overall GPA, can help determine whether a student needs to take remedial courses or is prepared for college-level classes.",
    "AB 705 allows students to move through college at a rate that matches their potential and increases their likelihood for success.",
    "80% of students entering community college in California are deemed underprepared and referred to at least one remedial course in math or English.",
    "The success rates for Oxnard Community College students beginning in remedial courses are just 34% for English and 27% for math.",
    "Placement tests alone have been poor predictors of a student's success according to national research by Columbia University.",
    "Using multiple measures can significantly reduce remediation rates."
  ]
}
```
